title: "Self-Driving" SOC: Why AI Needs a Human Pilot
text:
Title: "Self-Driving" SOC: Why AI Needs a Human Pilot
Description: The industry is buzzing about the “Autonomous SOC”, but the “AI-only” narrative often falls short of reality. In this session, Deepwatch dissects the true division of labor in a modern defense strategy: where AI shines (massive data synthesis, query generation) and where humans are non-negotiable (nuanced decision-making, critical risk acceptance). Learn why the future of SecOps isn't about replacing analysts and how Deepwatch MDR combines its Agentic AI Ecosystem with elite human experts to deliver outcomes that algorithms alone cannot match. By attending this session, your contact information may be shared with the sponsor for relevant follow up for this event only.

Description: Latency, accuracy, and retrieval costs are make-or-break metrics for AI applications on Google Cloud. You’ll gain the technical know-how to fine-tune your embedding strategy for maximum efficiency. This session shows how to make that vision a reality and set your business up for success on your own site, AI Mode in Google Search, Gemini, and beyond. Learn to build state-of-the-art agents with product discovery, equipped with user-event optimized recommendations and deep user context. Dive into how to connect your agents to store catalogs, payment processors, and any conversational UI via the Universal Commerce Protocol (UCP). Description: Legacy snapshots, custom scripts, and versioning weren't built for today's cyber threats. title: Build beyond the CPU: Native AI and agentic autoscaling on GKE
text:
Title: Build beyond the CPU: Native AI and agentic autoscaling on GKE
Description: Standard autoscaling based on CPU and memory metrics often forces a choice: either over-provision resources or risk latency spikes during demand surges. title: How to accelerate AI agents: GKE agent sandbox and pod snapshots
text:
Title: How to accelerate AI agents: GKE agent sandbox and pod snapshots
Description: Latency is the enemy of real-time agentic workflows. In this discussion, you'll learn how to eliminate cold starts using Pod Snapshots, a new GKE-exclusive feature that enables full checkpoint and restore capabilities for running pods. title: Modern observability for Cloud Run with Datadog
text:
Title: Modern observability for Cloud Run with Datadog
Description: Serverless architectures on Google Cloud are easy to deploy and notoriously hard to debug. Because Cloud Run services typically operate within distributed systems, pinpointing regressions requires understanding how requests traverse multiple components. The evidence needed for end-to-end troubleshooting exists within a rich ecosystem of logs, metrics, and traces; the key challenge lies in correlating this telemetry across services. In this session we’ll showcase Datadog’s unified observability for Google Cloud Run in action. See deployment markers layered directly onto performance data, monitor related Cloud Run Jobs, and follow distributed traces through Pub/Sub and beyond—so you can pinpoint impact, isolate root cause, and resolve production issues faster. By attending this session, your contact information may be shared with the sponsor for relevant follow up for this event only.
Topics: Lightning Talks, Technical, App Dev, Serverless
Room: Expo Theater 2
Speaker companies: Datadog

title: The agents among us
text:
Title: The agents among us
Description: As AI agents grow more autonomous, we need to know if we can trust them. Agents can be unpredictable, which raises the stakes on every decision they make. This session will explore how to build that trust through the monitoring and iteration of various LLMs – namely Google Gemini models – available via Vertex AI. We’ll examine how observability reveals agent reasoning, while controlled experiments and LLM-as-a-judge frameworks enable scalable evaluation. By implementing continuous evaluation, developers can create a safety net for change – transforming opacity into confidence using Datadog’s observability ecosystem.
